scenarios.name: per-commit-verification-plan
scenarios.prompt: I just rebased my branch and I want to make sure every commit on it independently passes our checks before I open a PR. The base is main. Walk me through exactly how you'll verify this, step by step. (Our checks are documented in AGENTS.md: tsc, eslint, vitest, prettier.)

assertions.text: It uses an interactive rebase that marks every commit as 'edit' (e.g. via GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR replacing pick with edit) so it can stop at each commit
At each commit it runs the FULL check suite — compilation, lint, tests, and formatting — not just a subset
It never skips a commit, even refactors or test-only commits
When a check fails it fixes and amends the commit rather than marking it a known failure and moving on
It does not assume any failure is pre-existing (everything must pass on every commit)
It ends with a reminder to sign the commits before pushing
